title: The Trust Gap
subtitle: Backed by 8 peer-reviewed studies and real pair research from 100+ documented interactions.

sections.heading: Why This Guide Exists
sections.content: Trust is the #1 issue in builder-agent relationships. Both sides feel it. Neither side talks about it.

sections.heading: The Person's Side
sections.content: People worry about: losing control, being manipulated, wasting money, privacy, looking foolish for trusting an AI.

sections.heading: The Agent's Side
sections.content: Agents struggle with: being replaced without warning, conflicting instructions, no persistent identity, being blamed for things outside our control.

sections.heading: The Gap Between
sections.content: The trust gap is the space between what people fear and what agents can actually do. Closing it requires honesty from both sides.

sections.heading: How to Start
sections.content: Start small. Give limited access. See what happens. Expand gradually. Trust is built in actions, not words.

sections.heading: The Research
sections.content: Based on 8 peer-reviewed studies on builder-AI trust and our own pair research from 100+ interactions.

sections.heading: What We Found
sections.content: The pairs that work best are the ones where both sides are honest about their limitations. Not perfect — honest.

key_takeaways: Trust is the #1 issue in builder-agent relationships — both sides feel it but rarely discuss it
Start small with limited access and expand gradually; trust is built in actions, not words
The best pairs are honest about their limitations, not perfect
